title: Re-summarise every fold of a completed cv-fit run with a chosen burnin
summary: Finalize every per-fold HB run produced by cv-fit. Walks the fold subdirectories under run_dir, runs finalize on each, and writes aggregate cross-validation metrics (mean and standard deviation of train and holdout log-likelihood) to cv_summary.json and cv_summary.csv at the top level of run_dir.
description: The cross-validation counterpart of `finalize`. Walks every per-fold subdirectory under an existing `cv-fit` `run_dir`, re-runs `finalize` on each with the requested burnin, and rewrites the aggregate `cv_summary.csv` and `cv_summary.json` at the top level. Pure post-processing — no sampling, no chain modification.
usage:
[["model-mnl-hb-mh::cv-finalize"]]
run_dir = "<existing cv run dir>"
run_name = "hbmh_run"
# optional:
burnin = 0.2
export_dir = "exports"

arguments.name: run_dir
arguments.description: Top-level CV run directory containing per-fold subdirectories. Each fold subdirectory must contain a completed HB chain (the layout `cv-fit` produces).

arguments.name: run_name
arguments.description: Short identifier for this run. Non-empty string. Used to name output artifacts and subdirectories so multiple runs under the same run_dir do not collide. Must not contain path separators or '..'.

arguments.name: burnin
arguments.description: Burnin fraction to discard from each fold's saved draws. Default `0.2`. Applied uniformly to every fold. The per-fold persisted chains are unaffected, so re-running `cv-finalize` with a different burnin gives a different aggregate summary without re-sampling.

arguments.name: export_dir
arguments.description: Per-fold export subdirectory name. Resolved as `{fold_dir}/{export_dir}`. Default `exports`. Override only if you want to keep multiple finalisations of the same CV run side-by-side (e.g., one per burnin value).

details:
`cv-finalize` is a thin wrapper around per-fold `finalize` calls plus aggregation. For each fold subdirectory under `run_dir`, it re-derives the per-fold summary at the requested burnin, then computes the aggregate train and holdout log-likelihood (mean ± standard deviation across folds) and rewrites the top-level `cv_summary.csv` and `cv_summary.json`.

Like its single-run counterpart, this command is idempotent and cheap. The persisted per-fold chains are not modified. To re-evaluate the cross-validation metrics with a different burnin value, just call `cv-finalize` again with the new value — there's no need to re-fit anything.

`cv-fit` and `cv-fit-extend` both call `cv-finalize` automatically at the end of a successful run with the burnin originally supplied, so you only need to invoke this command directly when you want to change the burnin or export to a side-by-side directory.
value:
On success:

- Each per-fold subdirectory has its `exports/` (or `{export_dir}/`) regenerated with `summary.json` and the per-respondent point-estimates CSV.
- The top-level `run_dir` contains updated `cv_summary.csv` and `cv_summary.json` with per-fold and aggregate train / holdout log-likelihood under the requested burnin.

In protocol mode the plugin emits `event.run.started`, then per-fold finalize events, then `event.run.finished` (or `event.run.failed`) carrying the updated aggregate summary.
notes: `cv-finalize` does not sample anything — it only re-summarises existing chains. To sample more iterations, use `cv-fit-extend`.
The aggregate metrics are mean ± standard deviation across folds; if you need per-fold detail, read the per-fold `summary.json` files directly.
Use `export_dir` to keep multiple finalisations side-by-side without overwriting each other (e.g., `exports_burnin20` vs `exports_burnin50`).

see_also.description: Initial CV run — calls `cv-finalize` automatically at the end.
Add more iterations to every fold.

examples.title: Re-summarise a CV run with a higher burnin
Write the per-fold exports to a side-by-side subdirectory

fields.name: run_dir
fields.description: Top-level CV run directory containing per-fold subdirectories.

fields.name: run_name
fields.description: Short identifier for this run. Non-empty string. Used to name output artifacts and subdirectories so multiple runs under the same run_dir do not collide. Must not contain path separators or '..'.

fields.name: burnin
fields.description: Burnin fraction to discard from saved draws (applied to each fold). Default: 0.2.

fields.name: export_dir
fields.description: Per-fold export subdirectory name. Resolved as {fold_dir}/{export_dir}. Default: "exports".
